text:
Once upon a time in a forest far, far away, there lived a curious squirrel named Tibbles. Tibbles loved exploring the woods. Each morning, he would scamper out of his leaf bed, nibbling on acorn crumbs left behind by careless humans, and venture into the heart of the forest.

It was a peaceful place. The sun painted the trees with golden light, and birdsong filled the air. Tibbles often found interesting things to explore. One day, while nibbling on an unusual berry, he discovered something peculiar — a tiny talking mouse!

The mouse introduced himself as Faye. He didn't talk much, but Tibbles could understand him. Faye complained about the squirrels stealing his acorns. Tibbles felt guilty.

"Why?" asked Tibbles.

"Why?" Faye repeated. "Because you eat my precious acorns?"

"Oh," Tibbles said sheepishly.

Faye smiled sadly. "There are lots of other foods around. I just seem to fit
